sn35.6:1.1: "Bhikkhus, forms are nonself. What is nonself should be seen as it really is with correct wisdom thus: ‘This is not mine, this I am not, this is not my self.’
sn35.6:1.2: "Sounds … Odours … Tastes … Tactile objects … Mental phenomena are nonself. What is nonself should be seen as it really is with correct wisdom thus: ‘This is not mine, this I am not, this is not my self.’
sn35.6:1.3: "Seeing thus … He understands: ‘ … there is no more for this state of being.’"sn.iv.4
